messages.content: hOW to convert 45.67890 in 45.7 in google sheets

In Google Sheets, if you want to display the number 45.67890 as 45.7, you would round the number to one decimal place. You can achieve this using the ROUND function. Here's how:

1. Suppose the value 45.67890 is in cell A1.
2. In a new cell where you want to display the rounded value, type the formula:
   `=ROUND(A1, 1)`

This formula rounds the number in cell A1 to 1 decimal place, which will give you the result 45.7.

Alternatively, if you simply want to change the display format without altering the data, you can use the toolbar formatting options:

1. Click on the cell that contains the value 45.67890.
2. Go to the Format menu, choose 'Number', and then select 'Custom number format...'
3. In the custom number format dialogue, enter `0.0` into the text box and press "Apply."

The cell will now display the number with only one decimal place (45.7), even though the underlying value will still be 45.67890.
Sort column but not title on sheets

To sort a column in Google Sheets without including the title (or header), follow these steps:

1. Click on the cell below the header in the column you want to sort. For example, if your header is in the first row and you want to sort column A, click on cell A2.
2. Hold down the Shift key, then click on the last cell in the column with data that you want to include in the sort. This will select the range that you want to sort without including the header.
3. Once the range is selected, go to the "Data" menu at the top of the page.
4. Choose either "Sort range by column A-Z" or "Sort range by column Z-A" (depending on whether you want an ascending or descending sort) and the corresponding letter for your column.
   - For an ascending sort: "Sort range A → Z"
   - For a descending sort: "Sort range Z → A"

Make sure the selection does not include the header row.

Alternatively, you can use the "Sort range" option for more control:

1. Select the range you want to sort (excluding the header).
2. Go to the "Data" menu at the top of the page.
3. Click on "Sort range" instead of "Sort range A–Z" or "Sort range Z–A".
4. In the pop-up window, select the column you want to sort by and choose whether it should be sorted A→Z or Z→A (ascending or descending).
5. Make sure the "Data has header row" option is unchecked since you are not selecting the header.
6. Click "Sort."

This will sort the selected range, leaving the header row in place.
How do i set my header row

Google Sheets allows you to designate a row as a header (also known as a "frozen" row) so that it remains visible as you scroll through the sheet. Here's how you can freeze the first row as a header:

1. Open your Google Sheets spreadsheet.
2. Click on the row number of the row you want to set as the header to highlight the entire row. Usually, this is row 1.
3. Go to the "View" menu.
4. Hover over the "Freeze" option in the drop-down menu.
5. Select "1 row" from the submenu. (If you wish to freeze more than one row, for example, the first two rows, then select "2 rows" instead.)

After you complete these steps, you'll notice a thicker grey line appears underneath the frozen row(s), indicating that the row has been set as the header. Now, when you scroll down through your sheet, the top row(s) will remain visible.

Alternatively, you can freeze the header row using the separator bar:

1. Locate the gray bar that separates the row numbers from the sheet's grid. It will be right above the row numbers on the left side.
2. Hover over the gray bar until your cursor turns into a hand icon.
3. Click and drag the gray bar downward to cover the number of rows you want to freeze.
4. Release the mouse button when the desired row(s) are highlighted.

The rows above the bar will now be frozen and will remain visible as you scroll through the spreadsheet.
